en: He also said to the disciples, "There was a rich man who had a manager, and charges were brought to him that this man was wasting his possessions.
zh: 耶穌又對門徒說：“有一個財主的管家，有人在他主人面前告他浪費主人的財物。

en: And he called him and said to him, 'What is this that I hear about you? Turn in the account of your management, for you can no longer be manager.'
zh: 主人把他叫來，對他說：‘我聽見關於你的是怎麼一回事呢？把你管理的帳目交代清楚，因為你不能再作管家了。’

en: And the manager said to himself, 'What shall I do, since my master is taking the management away from me? I am not strong enough to dig, and I am ashamed to beg.
zh: 那管家心裡說：‘主人不要我再作管家了，我作甚麼才好呢？鋤地吧，沒有力氣！討飯吧，怕羞！

en: I have decided what to do, so that when I am removed from management, people may receive me into their houses.'
zh: 我知道要怎麼作了，好使人在我離開管家的職務以後，接我到他們家裡去。’

en: So, summoning his master's debtors one by one, he said to the first, 'How much do you owe my master?'
zh: 於是把欠他主人債的人，一個一個地叫來，問頭一個說：‘你欠我主人多少？’

en: He said, 'A hundred measures of oil.' He said to him, 'Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.'
zh: 他說：‘四千公升油。’管家說：‘拿你的欠單去，快坐下寫二千。’

en: Then he said to another, 'And how much do you owe?' He said, 'A hundred measures of wheat.' He said to him, 'Take your bill, and write eighty.'
zh: 又問一個說：‘你欠多少？’他說：‘四萬公升麥子。’管家說：‘拿你的欠單去，寫三萬。’

en: The master commended the dishonest manager for his shrewdness. For the sons of this world are more shrewd in dealing with their own generation than the sons of light.
zh: 主人就誇獎這不義的管家作事機警；因為在應付自己的世事上，今世之子比光明之子更精明。

en: And I tell you, make friends for yourselves by means of unrighteous wealth, so that when it fails they may receive you into the eternal dwellings.
zh: 我告訴你們，要用不義的錢財 去結交朋友，好叫錢財無用的時候，他們可以接你們到永恆的帳棚裡。

en: "One who is faithful in a very little is also faithful in much, and one who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
zh: 在最小的事上忠心的，在大事上也忠心；在最小的事上不義的，在大事上也不義。

en: If then you have not been faithful in the unrighteous wealth, who will entrust to you the true riches?
zh: 如果你們在不義的錢財上不忠心，誰還把真實的錢財託付給你們呢？

en: And if you have not been faithful in that which is another's, who will give you that which is your own?
zh: 如果你們在別人的東西上不忠心，誰還把你們自己的東西交給你們呢？

en: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and money."
zh: “一個家僕不能服事兩個主人；他若不是恨這個愛那個，就是忠於這個輕視那個。你們不能服事 神，又服事金錢。”

en: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all these things, and they ridiculed him.
zh: 貪財的法利賽人聽見這些話，就嗤笑耶穌。

en: And he said to them, "You are those who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts. For what is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.
zh: 耶穌對他們說：“你們在人面前自稱為義， 神卻知道你們的心；因為人所高舉的， 神卻看作是可憎惡的。

en: "The Law and the Prophets were until John; since then the good news of the kingdom of God is preached, and everyone forces his way into it.
zh: 律法和先知到約翰就結束了。從此， 神的國的福音就傳開了，人人都要努力進去。

en: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one dot of the Law to become void.
zh: 天地過去，要比律法的一點一畫失效還容易呢。

en: "Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and he who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
zh: 凡休妻另娶的，就犯了姦淫；娶被丈夫所休的，也是犯了姦淫。

en: "There was a rich man who was clothed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day.
zh: “有一個財主，身穿紫色袍和細麻衣，天天奢華宴樂。

en: And at his gate was laid a poor man named Lazarus, covered with sores,
zh: 又有一個乞丐，名叫拉撒路，滿身是瘡，被人放在財主門口，

en: who desired to be fed with what fell from the rich man's table. Moreover, even the dogs came and licked his sores.
zh: 想得財主桌子上掉下來的零碎充飢；並且有狗來舔他的瘡。

en: The poor man died and was carried by the angels to Abraham's side. The rich man also died and was buried,
zh: 後來乞丐死了，被天使送到亞伯拉罕的懷裡。那財主也死了，並且埋葬了。

en: and in Hades, being in torment, he lifted up his eyes and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side.
zh: 財主在陰間受痛苦，舉目遠遠望見亞伯拉罕，和他懷裡的拉撒路，

en: And he called out, 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the end of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am in anguish in this flame.'
zh: 就喊著說：‘我祖亞伯拉罕啊，可憐我吧！打發拉撒路來用指頭蘸點水，涼涼我的舌頭吧！因為我在這火燄裡非常痛苦。’

en: But Abraham said, 'Child, remember that you in your lifetime received your good things, and Lazarus in like manner bad things; but now he is comforted here, and you are in anguish.
zh: 亞伯拉罕說：‘孩子，你應該回想你生前享過福，同樣拉撒路受過苦，現在他在這裡得安慰，你卻要受苦了。

en: And besides all this, between us and you a great chasm has been fixed, in order that those who would pass from here to you may not be able, and none may cross from there to us.'
zh: 不但這樣，我們與你們之間，有深淵隔開，人想從這邊過到你們那裡是不可能的，從那邊過到我們這邊也是不可能的。’

en: And he said, 'Then I beg you, father, to send him to my father's house-
zh: 那人說：‘我祖啊，那麼求你差遣拉撒路到我家裡去，

en: for I have five brothers- so that he may warn them, lest they also come into this place of torment.'
zh: 因為我有五個兄弟，他可以警告他們，免得他們也到這受苦的地方來。’

en: But Abraham said, 'They have Moses and the Prophets; let them hear them.'
zh: 亞伯拉罕說：‘他們有摩西和先知可以聽從。’

en: And he said, 'No, father Abraham, but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.'
zh: 他說：‘不然，我祖亞伯拉罕啊，如果有人從死人中復活，到他們那裡去，他們必定會悔改。’

en: He said to him, 'If they do not hear Moses and the Prophets, neither will they be convinced if someone should rise from the dead.'"
zh: 亞伯拉罕說：‘如果他們不聽從摩西和先知，就算有一個從死人中復活的人，他們也不會接受勸告。’”
